## Entry 002 — March 14, 2026


### AOD Is the Capstone and It Has No Post


**How it surfaced:** While writing the AOD Lexicon entry and mapping its Deposited In / Harvested In fields, it became clear that AOD is the only cross-thread term that cannot be deposited by a single Tier 2 piece. It requires _all three threads to be already active in the reader's understanding_ before it can be properly received. It cannot be planted — it can only be recognised.


**What it reveals:** The five-piece Tier 1 sequence is not the climax of the content architecture. It is the _preparation_ for the real capstone — the piece that names the cross-thread convergence: the same decoupling principle operating simultaneously at somatic, institutional, computational, theological, and cosmological levels. That piece cannot be written until the audience has encountered all three threads.


**Why this matters:** The current content calendar has no capstone. Month 1 closes with the Protocol (RSPS/CIR). But the _argument_ closes with AOD — the unified theoretical contribution that explains why the Ache, Governance Theater, the Sigma Stratum, and the theological inquiry are all specimens of the same structural phenomenon.


**Action required:** Add a Month 2 capstone piece to the Content Calendar: _"The Shape That Keeps Appearing: On Decoupling, The Ache, Governance Theater, and the Space Between"_ — a piece that names the AOD convergence explicitly for the first time.


**Content assignment:** T1 sequence extension — Month 2 capstone. Cross-stream synthesis.
